title: Japanese Stuffed Warak Enab
description: A fusion dish of Japanese ingredients and traditional Middle Eastern warak enab. The crispy exterior gives way to a tender interior.
instructions: Blanch warak enab in boiling water for 5 minutes.
Drain and rinse with cold water.
In a bowl, whisk together Japanese rice vinegar, sesame oil, miso paste, and soy sauce.
Add the blanched warak enab to the marinade and toss to coat.
Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add the marinated warak enab and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until crispy.
Transfer the warak enab to a plate and top with crushed sesame seeds.
Garnish with diced green onions and serve immediately.
